title: Systems
summary: This page concludes the discussion of even/odd signal decomposition by extending it to complex signals using conjugate symmetry and conjugate-antisymmetry, providing formulas for decomposing any signal into conjugate-symmetric and conjugate-antisymmetric parts. It then introduces the concept of systems, describing them as physical or software entities that process signals, governed by terminal relationships and interconnection laws. The page outlines the three major areas of systems study: mathematical modeling, analysis, and design, and introduces the black-box representation of a system with multiple inputs and outputs.

figures.caption: A multiple-input, multiple-output (MIMO) system with j inputs x₁(t) through xⱼ(t) and k outputs y₁(t) through yₖ(t).
figures.description: The figure is a block diagram with no axes, showing a central rectangular system block. On the left, multiple input signals labeled x₁(t), x₂(t), …, xⱼ(t) enter the block with arrows pointing right; on the right, multiple output signals labeled y₁(t), y₂(t), …, yₖ(t) leave the block with arrows pointing right. The vertical dots indicate additional channels between the first and last labeled signals. Students should notice that this represents a multiple-input, multiple-output system where several time-varying inputs are mapped to several outputs.
keywords: inputs x1 t to xj t
outputs y1 t to yk t
